slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 2 - Research Methodology
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'uses facts or information already available and analyzes them to make a critical evaluation of the material.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Analytical Research** is defined as: uses facts or information already available and analyzes them to make a critical evaluation of the material. (critical factual evaluation).

slides.question: Q2: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is related to some abstract idea(s) or theory, generally used by philosophers and thinkers to develop new concepts or to re-interpret existing ones.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Conceptual Research** is defined as: is related to some abstract idea(s) or theory, generally used by philosophers and thinkers to develop new concepts or to re-interpret existing ones. (abstract concept re-interpretation).

slides.question: Q3: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a category of research design in which the investigation starts after the fact has occurred without interference from the researcher.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Ex-Post Facto Research** is defined as: is a category of research design in which the investigation starts after the fact has occurred without interference from the researcher. (after-the-fact investigation).

slides.question: Q4: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is an observational research method in which data is gathered for the same subjects repeatedly over a period of time.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Longitudinal Study** is defined as: is an observational research method in which data is gathered for the same subjects repeatedly over a period of time. (long-term repeated observation).

slides.question: Q5: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'involves looking at data from a population at one specific point in time to analyze prevalence or differences.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Cross-Sectional Study** is defined as: involves looking at data from a population at one specific point in time to analyze prevalence or differences. (single point-in-time snapshot).

slides.question: Q6: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is an in-depth, detailed study of an individual, group, or event to explore underlying principles in a real-life context.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Case Study Method** is defined as: is an in-depth, detailed study of an individual, group, or event to explore underlying principles in a real-life context. (in-depth single unit analysis).

slides.question: Q7: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'establishes cause-and-effect relationships by manipulating an independent variable and observing its effect on a dependent variable.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Experimental Research** is defined as: establishes cause-and-effect relationships by manipulating an independent variable and observing its effect on a dependent variable. (cause-and-effect verification).

slides.question: Q8: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'determines the frequency with which something occurs or its association with something else, aiming to find causes of a phenomenon.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Diagnostic Research** is defined as: determines the frequency with which something occurs or its association with something else, aiming to find causes of a phenomenon. (cause-and-association diagnosis).

slides.question: Q9: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a systematic assessment of the worth or merit of a time-bound project, program, or policy.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Evaluative Research** is defined as: is a systematic assessment of the worth or merit of a time-bound project, program, or policy. (worth and outcome assessment).

slides.question: Q10: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'focuses on identifying similarities and differences between two or more groups, subjects, or datasets.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Comparative Research** is defined as: focuses on identifying similarities and differences between two or more groups, subjects, or datasets. (similarity and difference analysis).

slides.question: Q11: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a variable that is kept constant or controlled throughout the experiment to prevent it from influencing the outcome.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Control Variable** is defined as: is a variable that is kept constant or controlled throughout the experiment to prevent it from influencing the outcome. (constant baseline variable).

slides.question: Q12: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a comprehensive summary of previous research on a specific topic, identifying gaps in current knowledge.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Literature Review** is defined as: is a comprehensive summary of previous research on a specific topic, identifying gaps in current knowledge. (critical gap analysis of past works).

slides.question: Q13: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a clear, precise statement identifying an area of concern, a condition to be improved, or a difficulty to be eliminated.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Research Problem** is defined as: is a clear, precise statement identifying an area of concern, a condition to be improved, or a difficulty to be eliminated. (clear problem identification).

slides.question: Q14: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a visual or written product that explains, either graphically or in narrative form, the main things to be studied.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Conceptual Framework** is defined as: is a visual or written product that explains, either graphically or in narrative form, the main things to be studied. (conceptual schema explanation).

slides.question: Q15: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a small-scale preliminary study conducted to evaluate feasibility, time, cost, and adverse events prior to a full-scale research project.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Pilot Study** is defined as: is a small-scale preliminary study conducted to evaluate feasibility, time, cost, and adverse events prior to a full-scale research project. (feasibility and scale trial).

slides.question: Q16: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is selected when the primary objective is to gain insights and familiarity with a problem for more precise investigation later.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Exploratory Design** is defined as: is selected when the primary objective is to gain insights and familiarity with a problem for more precise investigation later. (insight and familiarity collection).

slides.question: Q17: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is chosen to provide an accurate portrayal of characteristics of a particular individual, situation, or group.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Descriptive Design** is defined as: is chosen to provide an accurate portrayal of characteristics of a particular individual, situation, or group. (accurate characteristic profiling).

slides.question: Q18: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the group in an experiment that does not receive the treatment or manipulation, serving as a baseline for comparison.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Control Group** is defined as: is the group in an experiment that does not receive the treatment or manipulation, serving as a baseline for comparison. (untreated baseline group).

slides.question: Q19: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a research design that shares similarities with traditional experimental design but lacks random assignment to groups.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Quasi-Experimental Design** is defined as: is a research design that shares similarities with traditional experimental design but lacks random assignment to groups. (non-randomized comparison design).

slides.question: Q20: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is an experimental procedure in which neither the participants nor the researchers know who is receiving a particular treatment.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Double-Blind Study** is defined as: is an experimental procedure in which neither the participants nor the researchers know who is receiving a particular treatment. (participant-researcher bias prevention).

slides.question: Q21: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'relies on existing research participants to recruit future participants from among their acquaintances.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Snowball Sampling** is defined as: relies on existing research participants to recruit future participants from among their acquaintances. (referral-based chain network).

slides.question: Q22: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'occurs when the list from which the sample is drawn does not accurately represent the target population.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Sampling Frame Error** is defined as: occurs when the list from which the sample is drawn does not accurately represent the target population. (frame-population mismatch error).

slides.question: Q23: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'occurs when some members of a population are systematically more likely to be selected in a sample than others.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Sampling Bias** is defined as: occurs when some members of a population are systematically more likely to be selected in a sample than others. (systematic selection skew).

slides.question: Q24: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is data collected firsthand by the researcher specifically for the current research project.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Primary Data** is defined as: is data collected firsthand by the researcher specifically for the current research project. (original direct collection).

slides.question: Q25: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is pre-existing data collected by others for a different purpose, such as census data or published articles.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Secondary Data** is defined as: is pre-existing data collected by others for a different purpose, such as census data or published articles. (previously collected material).

slides.question: Q26: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a structured research instrument consisting of a series of questions for gathering information from respondents.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Questionnaire** is defined as: is a structured research instrument consisting of a series of questions for gathering information from respondents. (standardized self-report tool).

slides.question: Q27: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is an interview method where all questions are pre-determined and asked in exactly the same order for all respondents.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Structured Interview** is defined as: is an interview method where all questions are pre-determined and asked in exactly the same order for all respondents. (rigid standardized interview).

slides.question: Q28: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is an interval scale used to measure attitudes by asking respondents to specify their level of agreement on a symmetric agree-disagree scale.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Likert Scale** is defined as: is an interval scale used to measure attitudes by asking respondents to specify their level of agreement on a symmetric agree-disagree scale. (measuring ordinal/interval agreement levels).

slides.question: Q29: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a scaling method that measures the psychological meaning of concepts using bipolar adjectives (e.g., Good-Bad).'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Semantic Differential Scale** is defined as: is a scaling method that measures the psychological meaning of concepts using bipolar adjectives (e.g., Good-Bad). (bipolar adjective scaling).

slides.question: Q30: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'involves gathering primary data by systematically watching and recording behavioral patterns of people, objects, or occurrences.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Observation Method** is defined as: involves gathering primary data by systematically watching and recording behavioral patterns of people, objects, or occurrences. (systematic behavior recording).

slides.question: Q31: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a non-parametric test used to determine if there is a significant association between two categorical variables.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Chi-Square Test** is defined as: is a non-parametric test used to determine if there is a significant association between two categorical variables. (categorical association verification).

slides.question: Q32: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a parametric test used to compare the means of three or more independent groups.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **ANOVA (Analysis of Variance)** is defined as: is a parametric test used to compare the means of three or more independent groups. (comparing three or more group means).

slides.question: Q33: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a parametric test used to compare the means of exactly two groups, either independent or paired.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Student's t-Test** is defined as: is a parametric test used to compare the means of exactly two groups, either independent or paired. (comparing two group means).

slides.question: Q34: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a statistical test used to compare means when the sample size is large (n > 30) and population variance is known.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Z-Test** is defined as: is a statistical test used to compare means when the sample size is large (n > 30) and population variance is known. (large-sample mean comparison).

slides.question: Q35: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the probability of rejecting the null hypothesis when it is true, commonly set at 0.05.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Level of Significance** is defined as: is the probability of rejecting the null hypothesis when it is true, commonly set at 0.05. (alpha threshold for hypothesis rejection).

slides.question: Q36: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a range of values derived from sample statistics that is likely to contain the true population parameter.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Confidence Interval** is defined as: is a range of values derived from sample statistics that is likely to contain the true population parameter. (parameter estimation range).

slides.question: Q37: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'indicates the number of values in the final calculation of a statistic that are free to vary.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Degrees of Freedom** is defined as: indicates the number of values in the final calculation of a statistic that are free to vary. (independent observation count limit).

slides.question: Q38: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'measures the standard deviation of the sample mean's estimate of the population mean.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Standard Error of the Mean** is defined as: measures the standard deviation of the sample mean's estimate of the population mean. (sample mean precision estimate).

slides.question: Q39: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a symmetric, bell-shaped probability distribution where the mean, median, and mode are all equal.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Normal Distribution** is defined as: is a symmetric, bell-shaped probability distribution where the mean, median, and mode are all equal. (symmetric bell-curve model).

slides.question: Q40: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a distribution with a tail extending toward the right side (higher values), where Mean > Median > Mode.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Positively Skewed** is defined as: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the right side (higher values), where Mean > Median > Mode. (right-tail distribution skew).

slides.question: Q41: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a committee formally designated to approve, monitor, and review biomedical and behavioral research involving humans.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Institutional Review Board (IRB)** is defined as: is a committee formally designated to approve, monitor, and review biomedical and behavioral research involving humans. (human subject safety oversight board).

slides.question: Q42: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the process of providing research participants with clear info about the study to help them voluntarily agree to participate.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Informed Consent** is defined as: is the process of providing research participants with clear info about the study to help them voluntarily agree to participate. (voluntary participant agreement process).

slides.question: Q43: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the evaluation of creative work or performance by other people in the same field in order to maintain quality.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Peer Review** is defined as: is the evaluation of creative work or performance by other people in the same field in order to maintain quality. (expert quality check before publication).

slides.question: Q44: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'refers to online, free-of-charge access to peer-reviewed scholarly research, removing price and permission barriers.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Open Access** is defined as: refers to online, free-of-charge access to peer-reviewed scholarly research, removing price and permission barriers. (barrier-free research accessibility).

slides.question: Q45: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a list of all sources consulted during the research process, regardless of whether they were directly cited in the text.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Bibliography** is defined as: is a list of all sources consulted during the research process, regardless of whether they were directly cited in the text. (consulted sources comprehensive list).

slides.question: Q46: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a self-contained, brief summary of a research paper, thesis, or review, outlining goals, methods, and main findings.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Abstract** is defined as: is a self-contained, brief summary of a research paper, thesis, or review, outlining goals, methods, and main findings. (brief paper summary overview).

slides.question: Q47: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a note placed at the bottom of a page of a document that provides additional context or citations for text on that page.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Footnote** is defined as: is a note placed at the bottom of a page of a document that provides additional context or citations for text on that page. (bottom-of-page contextual note).

slides.question: Q48: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a structured document outlining what the researcher plans to study, why it is important, and how they will execute it.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Research Proposal** is defined as: is a structured document outlining what the researcher plans to study, why it is important, and how they will execute it. (planned research blueprint outline).

slides.question: Q49: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a specialist work of writing on a single scholarly subject or aspect of a subject, usually by a single author.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Research Monograph** is defined as: is a specialist work of writing on a single scholarly subject or aspect of a subject, usually by a single author. (single-subject specialist writing piece).

slides.question: Q50: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the process of indexing publications by their citations to track scientific discourse and calculate metrics.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Citation Indexing** is defined as: is the process of indexing publications by their citations to track scientific discourse and calculate metrics. (tracking scientific citations system).

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 2. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Research Methodology Mock Exam 2.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Research Methodology questions!
